title: valeriana officinalis subsp. procurrens
intro: Valeriana officinalis subsp. procurrens, also known as the procumbent valerian, is a subspecies of the valerian plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Valeriana officinalis subsp. procurrens lie in its roots, which contain valepotriates and valerenic acid, compounds with sedative, anti-anxiety, and muscle relaxant effects. The health benefits of the plant include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety levels, and relief from muscle and joint pain. Valeriana officinalis subsp. procurrens can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
